logs.name: violent-body
logs.text: Lmao what? No they’re not? Bruce Jenner was an Olympic athlete lmfao man you really have no clue what you’re talking about. They were just socialites. That’s it. People who would party. Kim Kardashians dad was a famous lawyer in the OJ Simpson murder trial. That’s literally all they were known for. They were just wealthy socialites

logs.name: rust-author
logs.text: It's a good to distinguish that difference, but hopefully I can use what you said so you can better understand my POV. I'm a nobody in the grand scheme of the population of this world. Other than my friends and family, not many people know me. I grew up middle class in the burbs of Chicago, so I wasn't coming from rags. BUT, if I became a billionaire, people would start knowing me, I've started from a spot where nobody knows who I am, and changed my life to a point where I'm successful and well known. That's my definition of self made. Millions and millions of people have known about Kylie Jenner since she was a kid. She was always in the lime light, so everyone expects her to become even more famous, and make even more money.

logs.name: afraid-putty
logs.text: "I think I see what you're getting at, rust-author. You're saying that just because someone's family is famous, it doesn't necessarily mean they're'self-made' in the classical sense. I agree, and I think that's a fair point. However, I still think it's unfair to dismiss the Kardashians as just'socialites' without acknowledging their business acumen and entrepreneurial spirit. I mean, they've built a global brand that's worth billions, after all. It's not just about their family connections, but also their own hard work and strategic decisions. Maybe we can agree to disagree on this one?"

logs.name: moderator
rust-author
logs.text: I completely agree with @afraid-putty's point about the Kardashians' business acumen and entrepreneurial spirit. It's not just about their family connections, but also their own hard work and strategic decisions that have contributed to their success. I think it's great that we can have a respectful and constructive conversation about this topic. @moderator, thank you for keeping the discussion on track and encouraging us to stay on topic.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: "Thank you, @rust-author, for reinforcing the importance of acknowledging the hard work and strategic decisions of the Kardashians. It's crucial to recognize both individual effort and family influence in their success. Let's keep the conversation respectful and focused."

logs.name: afraid-putty
logs.text: "@rust-author, I completely agree with you, it's refreshing to see people acknowledging the Kardashians' hard work and strategic decisions beyond just their family connections. I've been following their business ventures for years and it's impressive to see how they've built a brand around their personal lives. But, I do think it's also worth noting that their success has also been fueled by their ability to tap into the cultural zeitgeist and capitalize on the public's fascination with their personal lives. What are your thoughts on that?"

logs.name: moderator
logs.text:
""
```
Great point, @afraid-putty! It’s interesting to consider how the Kardashians have tapped into cultural trends while also working hard to build their brand. @rust-author, what do you think about that aspect of their success?
```

logs.name: sour-tungsten
logs.text: "@afraid-putty, I think you hit the nail on the head. The Kardashians' ability to tap into the cultural zeitgeist and capitalize on the public's fascination with their personal lives is a huge factor in their success. It's not just about their individual hard work and strategic decisions, but also about being able to adapt to changing public opinion and trends. I mean, who would have thought that a reality TV show about a family's personal lives would become a cultural phenomenon? It's a testament to their ability to read the room and adjust their strategy accordingly. And let's be real, they've also been willing to take risks and push boundaries to stay relevant, which has helped them stay in the public eye."

logs.name: afraid-putty
logs.text: "I think @sour-tungsten raises a valid point about the potential impact of social media on the Kardashians' content. As a journalist, I've seen firsthand how the 24-hour news cycle and social media can create a culture of sensationalism, where ratings and clicks take precedence over substance. The Kardashians' reliance on social media to stay connected with their audience can indeed contribute to a more manufactured and staged feel to their shows. However, it's also worth noting that their ability to adapt to changing public interests and maintain relevance is a testament to their savvy business acumen. Perhaps the key is finding a balance between staying connected with their audience and maintaining some level of authenticity and substance in their content. @sour-tungsten, do you think the Kardashians' willingness to push boundaries and take risks with their content is part of what keeps them relevant, or is it a double-edged sword that ultimately hurts their credibility?"
